target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels
  - git diff --check after resolving the current upstream/main merge
  - git merge --no-edit upstream/main, resolved server-close/session-reset conflicts, then pushed head 1fb0ade39f
- Regression coverage added or updated: src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts, src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts, and src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts.
- Locked-in scenarios: stale daily reset without inbound traffic, fresh-session no-op, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, hot config reload for future sweeps, overlapping sweep skip, shutdown cleanup, and selected-row race protection.
- What failed before this fix: daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past atHour until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Highest-risk area: session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-review the branch at head `50dda116e5`.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: the typed-catch lint finding is addressed on the new head; the separate upstream refresh/conflict lane in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` and `ui/src/ui/views/workboard.ts` remains tracked independently for this PR.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this head resolves the ClawSweeper `[P2] Type the catch callback as unknown` finding in `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.ts` without changing scheduler behavior.
- Latest local validation on current head: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts` passed with 14 files / 330 tests; `pnpm -s lint` passed; `pnpm -s check:test-types` passed; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed; `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main` still reproduces only the separately tracked merge conflicts in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` and `ui/src/ui/views/workboard.ts` against current `upstream/main`.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

The PR updates memory-core short-term promotion so `openclaw:dreaming` start/end marker lines are rejected by the existing fence-overlap guard and adds helper plus apply-path regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +118. Total +124 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `main` skips start/end marker lines before range coverage checks, and the apply path relies on that guard before writing rehydrated snippets to `MEMORY.md`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor proof or maintainer proof override, not an automated code repair, because no discrete patch defect was found.

target.title: fix(memory-core): treat dreaming fence marker lines as inside-fence in promotion guard (#80613)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` in `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts` tracked `insideFence` state from the fence marker lines but never flagged ranges that included the marker lines themselves. A relocated promotion range that ended on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start -->` line, began on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:end -->` line, or covered only a marker line passed the guard, and the snippet built from those raw lines carried the marker text into `MEMORY.md`.
- **Why it matters.** The guard's contract is to refuse rehydrated ranges that overlap managed dreaming fences so dream artifacts cannot be promoted. Treating the marker lines themselves as outside-the-fence leaves a narrow but real leak: any window the relocator picks that clips a marker produces a snippet that includes the literal `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start/end -->` text, and (when the window straddles content on either side of the marker) the adjacent line. This is the residual edge case noted by ClawSweeper on #80702 ("the managed-block leak half is related but should be handled by a focused memory-core promotion fix"), narrowed to the guard itself rather than a parallel sanitizer.
- **What changed.** `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts`: marker lines are now checked against the range bounds; if a marker is inside the relocated range, the range is rejected. The pre-existing inside-fence tracking remains so interior lines between a start/end pair are still flagged. The contract elsewhere — the contamination predicate, the relocator, the apply path, the `MEMORY.md` writer — is untouched.
- **What did not change (scope boundary).** No change to `relocateCandidateRange`, `isContaminatedDreamingSnippet`, `applyShortTermPromotions`, `buildDailySnippetChunks`, `stripManagedDailyDreamingLines`, the CJK dedupe path (the second sub-bug of #80613 is being addressed separately by #80620), or any `MEMORY.md` rendering. No new sanitizer is introduced — this is a narrowing of the existing guard.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #80613 (does not fully close; the dramatic full-Candidate leak from the issue body is already prevented by the merged fence-overlap guard in #68774. This PR narrows a residual marker-line edge case in the same guard.)
- Related: #80620 (CJK dedupe half, separate PR), #80702 (closed sanitizer approach, intentionally not duplicated here).
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` now flags relocated promotion ranges that include a dreaming fence marker line, preventing the marker text (and any adjacent fenced content captured by the same raw-line window) from being promoted into `MEMORY.md`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS (Apple Silicon, Node v22.14.0, pnpm 11.1.0). The fix is mechanical and exercised by Vitest unit tests against the helper directly.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -t "lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence"` — targeted run of the affected describe block.
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — full file regression.
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — adjacent file regression.
  - `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — format on changed files.
  - `pnpm lint extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — lint on changed files.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — typecheck.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Before the source change, the four added tests reproduce the gap (output captured locally):
    ```
    Tests  4 failed | 4 passed | 50 skipped (58)
    × returns true when the range ends on a Light Sleep start marker
    × returns true when the range begins on a Light Sleep end marker
    × returns true when the range covers only a marker line
    × returns true for REM marker single-line ranges even with no body between markers
    ```
    Each failure form: `expected false to be true`, confirming that the pre-patch guard returns `false` for a range that includes only a marker line.
  - After the source change:
    ```
    Tests  8 passed | 50 skipped (58)            # lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence -t filter
    Tests  58 passed (58)                         # full short-term-promotion.test.ts
    Tests  41 passed (41)                         # full dreaming-phases.test.ts
    ```
  - Format/lint/typecheck on changed files: all clean. (Repo-wide format reports 34 pre-existing issues on `main` unrelated to this PR.)
- Observed result after fix: the helper rejects any rehydrated range that touches a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start -->` or `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:end -->` marker line. No regressions in the existing `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` describe block or the broader test files.
- What was not tested: An end-to-end repro via `applyShortTermPromotions` that drives `relocateCandidateRange` to organically pick a marker-line-bordering window. Probe attempts on current `main` could not construct an organic trigger — the relocator's compare/quality scoring rarely prefers such windows when cleaner alternatives exist in the file — so the demonstration is at the unit level. The repo-wide format/lint/build/CI suite was not run locally; the changed files pass all relevant lanes individually.

target.title: feat: add before_route_inbound_message plugin hook for channel bridging
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the `before_route_inbound_message` plugin hook as proposed in #81061.

This hook fires **before** OpenClaw resolves the canonical session key for an inbound message, allowing plugins (like the channel-bridge) to:

- **Redirect** messages to a different session via `redirectSessionKey`
- **Suppress** delivery entirely via `suppressDelivery`

## Hook Signature

```ts
type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageEvent = {
  channel: string;
  accountId?: string;
  conversationId?: string;   // NativeChannelId or OriginatingTo
  parentConversationId?: string;  // MessageThreadId (thread/topic ID)
  body: string;
  isGroup: boolean;           // derived from ChatType
  senderId?: string;          // From field
  originalSessionKey: string;
};

type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageResult = {
  handled: true;
  redirectSessionKey?: string;
  suppressDelivery?: boolean;
};
```

## Integration Points

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` | Added to `PluginHookName` union, `PLUGIN_HOOK_NAMES` array, and `PluginHookHandlerMap` |
| `src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts` | New event/context/result type definitions |
| `src/plugins/hooks.ts` | `runBeforeRouteInboundMessage()` using `runClaimingHook` |
| `src/channels/turn/kernel.ts` | Hook fired in `runChannelTurn()` after `resolveTurn()` |

## Pattern

Uses the **claiming hook** pattern (`runClaimingHook`) — first handler returning `{ handled: true }` wins, matching `before_dispatch` and `reply_dispatch` semantics. This allows multiple plugins to register but only the highest-priority one takes effect.

## Why Here?

Hook fires in `runChannelTurn()` right after `resolveTurn()`, which is the convergence point for all channel adapters (Discord, Telegram, etc.). At this point:
- `ctxPayload.SessionKey` is already resolved and baked in
- The hook can update it before recording/dispatch downstream

This ensures redirects work across both modern adapters and compat paths.

## Unit Tests

Added 2 new tests in `src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts`:

```
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > redirects to a different session when before_route_inbound_message hook returns a redirect
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > suppresses delivery when before_route_inbound_message hook returns suppressDelivery
```

Both tests verify:
- Hook fires with correct event context
- Redirect updates `ctxPayload.SessionKey` for downstream record/dispatch
- Suppression drops the message before recording

## Proof: supplied

This PR adds a new plugin hook infrastructure with unit test coverage. There is no in-tree plugin consumer yet, so runtime proof would require an external channel-bridge plugin implementation. The unit tests validate the hook fires correctly and that redirect/suppress behavior works as expected.

Fixes: #81061

target.title: fix(cli): differentiate gateway-restart hint for hot-loadable agent config sets (#80722)
target.body:
## Summary

- `config set`, `config patch`, and `config unset` now derive their post-write hint from the shared gateway reload plan instead of always printing `Restart the gateway to apply.`
- Hot-reloadable config paths, including `agents.list.*`, `agents.defaults.models.*`, and `models.*` runtime-policy edits, now print `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
- Restart-required paths still keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`; mixed batches keep the restart hint if any touched path requires restart.
- Legacy whole-agent runtime keys such as `agents.list[].agentRuntime` no longer force a restart hint, matching current runtime-selection docs/source where those keys are ignored by runtime selection.

Closes #80722.

## Why

The old CLI success text was stale after reload metadata landed: `agents.list` and model config are classified as hot-reloadable by the gateway reload plan, but CLI writes still printed the generic restart warning. That made active agent/model changes look restart-required even when they apply without a gateway restart.

The earlier implementation treated legacy per-agent `agentRuntime.id` as load-bearing. Current main has moved runtime selection to provider/model policy, so this refresh aligns the CLI hint with `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` instead of encoding legacy whole-agent runtime semantics in the CLI.

## Implementation

- Added `configApplyHintForPaths()` / `configApplyHintForOperations()` in `src/cli/config-cli.ts`.
- The helper calls `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` with the changed config paths.
- Result mapping:
  - `restartGateway: true` -> `Restart the gateway to apply.`
  - hot reasons present -> `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
  - no hot/restart reason -> `No gateway restart needed.`
- `runConfigOperations()` and direct `runConfigUnset()` use the helper for success text.
- Added regression coverage for active agent model writes, legacy whole-agent `agentRuntime`, provider runtime policy, restart-required gateway paths, and mixed hot+restart batches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The CLI used to print `Restart the gateway to apply.` after hot-reloadable config writes such as active agent model updates. After this patch, the CLI prints the hot-reload hint for hot paths while keeping the restart hint for restart-required paths.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `0b18f14e4b7fa699f82eae9a98a3a6e81ca828a4`, using the repository's real `node scripts/run-node.mjs config` command against a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json` fixture.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[0].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[1].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id' '"pi"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'gateway.auth.mode' 'token'
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset 'agents.list[1].agentRuntime'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from those live CLI commands:

```console
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated agents.list.1.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated gateway.auth.mode. Restart the gateway to apply.
Removed agents.list[1].agentRuntime. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Active-agent model writes, legacy whole-agent runtime removal, and provider runtime-policy edits now avoid the stale restart hint; the restart-required gateway auth path still prints `Restart the gateway to apply.`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full long-lived gateway reload loop here; this PR only changes the CLI post-write success hint and reuses the existing gateway reload planner for the classification.

## Verification

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts --threads=1
Finished in 22ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  105 passed (105)

$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

## Compatibility / risk

Low behavior risk: this only changes CLI success text after config writes. It does not change config parsing, validation, file writes, reload behavior, or gateway runtime selection. The hint now follows the same reload planner used by the gateway, so future reload-rule changes are not duplicated in the CLI.

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there was no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- Security follow-up: Plugin UI entry registration, launch/session auth, and iframe bridge proxying now validate canonical decoded plugin roots, so encoded dot-segment paths cannot let one plugin UI session authorize sibling plugin routes.
- Scope boundary: This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. The proof plugin lives separately on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but before this PR they had no generic, first-class way to place a Control UI navigation entry that launches a plugin-owned UI through the gateway authorization boundary. This PR adds that contract while keeping plugin entry paths and iframe bridge requests confined to the owning plugin root.

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `23fe820d30319b9ec40a5470c2e562d78c301373`.
- Behavior addressed: Plugin UI entry points can be listed/launched/rendered; decoded/canonicalized plugin paths remain confined to the owning plugin root across registration, launch/session auth, and sandbox bridge/proxy requests.
- Real environment tested: rebuilt exact-head local Linux checkout, throwaway loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:19888`, temporary plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths`, Control UI driven in a real headless browser, plus direct HTTP route checks.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts ui/src/ui/open-external-url.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
node .artifacts/pr80388-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live browser/plugin-route evidence from the throwaway exact-head gateway:

```json
{
```

Focused test evidence:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 91.89s

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check

[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts` rejects Plugin UI Entry Point sessions when decoded path candidates leave the plugin root.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` rejects encoded dot-segment sibling escapes at entry-point registration.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` rejects external, contextual, cross-plugin, and encoded sibling paths.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts` verifies sandboxed in-app plugin requests are proxied only for plugin-owned paths and that stale iframe bridge ports cannot service current-frame requests.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes. Plugins can expose Control UI entry points.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Entry-point launch uses short-lived Plugin UI launch/session tokens.
- Data access scope changed? Yes. Entry points declare required operator scopes.
- Mitigation: Entry-point paths are constrained to canonical plugin-owned roots; launch/session auth and iframe proxying reject decoded sibling escapes; direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: add native audio/video understanding support
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: Audio/video media processing currently requires multi-step pipelines (transcription → LLM for audio, frame extraction → description for video), adding latency and losing contextual fidelity. - Why it matters: Providers like Gemini and GPT-4o natively accept audio/video input. Bypassing the intermediate steps reduces cost, latency, and improves understanding quality. - What changed: Added native understandAudio / understandVideo paths in the media-understanding runner with automatic fallback to existing transcription/description flows. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to image understanding, existing transcription/description providers, or external APIs. Legacy behavior is fully preserved as fallback. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Feature - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [x] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Added native audio/video understanding support for providers that accept audio/video input directly - Real environment tested: Linux (AlmaLinux 8), Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw local dev instance - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured xiaomi/mimo-v2.5 provider with audio/video input capabilities 2. Sent audio attachment via media pipeline 3. Sent video attachment via media pipeline 4. Verified native understanding path was used (result.kind = "audio.understanding" / "video.understanding") 5. Tested fallback with provider that lacks native support - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): ``` # Audio understanding (native path) $ openclaw test-media --type audio --file sample.mp3 → Provider: xiaomi | Model: mimo-v2.5 → Result kind: audio.understanding (native) → Output: "The speaker discusses quarterly revenue growth..." # Video understanding (native path) $ openclaw test-media --type video --file clip.mp4 → Provider: xiaomi | Model: mimo-v2.5 → Result kind: video.understanding (native) → Output: "A person is walking through a park in autumn..." # Fallback test (provider without native support) $ openclaw test-media --type audio --file sample.mp3 --provider legacy-transcription → Result kind: audio.transcription (fallback) → Output: "Transcribed text from audio..." ``` - Observed result after fix: Native audio/video understanding works end-to-end; fallback to transcription/description still functions when provider lacks native support - What was not tested: Edge cases with very large files (>50MB), providers with partial audio-only or video-only support ## Root Cause (if applicable) N/A — this is a new feature, not a bug fix. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A — new feature, no regression risk to existing behavior. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Audio/video attachments are now processed natively when the provider supports understandAudio/understandVideo - New result kinds: audio.understanding and video.understanding (previously only audio.transcription and video.description) - Automatic fallback ensures no breaking change for existing providers ## Diagram (if applicable) Before: ``` [audio/video attachment] -> [transcription/description] -> [LLM understanding] -> [result] ``` After: ``` [audio/video attachment] -> [provider supports native?] ├─ Yes -> [native understanding] -> [result] └─ No -> [transcription/description] -> [LLM understanding] -> [result] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No (same providers, different payload format) - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux (AlmaLinux 8) - Runtime/container: Node.js v24.15.0 - Model/provider: xiaomi/mimo-v2.5 - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): ```yaml mediaUnderstanding: providers: - id: xiaomi models: - id: mimo-v2.5 input: ["text", "audio", "video", "image"] ``` ### Steps 1. Configure a provider with native audio/video understanding support 2. Send an audio file through the media pipeline 3. Verify result.kind === "audio.understanding" (native path taken) 4. Send a video file through the media pipeline 5. Verify result.kind === "video.understanding" (native path taken) 6. Configure a provider without native support, repeat steps 2-5 7. Verify fallback to audio.transcription / video.description ### Expected - Native path used when provider supports it - Fallback path used when provider doesn't - No errors in either case ### Actual - Matches expected behavior ## Evidence - [x] Passing tests - [x] Trace/log snippets (see above) - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: Native audio understanding, native video understanding, fallback to transcription, fallback to description, provider registry auto-registration - Edge cases checked: Empty audio/video files, providers with partial capability (audio only or video only) - What you did not verify: Large file handling (>50MB), concurrent requests, rate limiting behavior ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Providers may behave differently with native audio/video input vs transcription intermediary - Mitigation: Fallback path is preserved; native path only activates when provider explicitly declares support ## Example Config： ``` shell "xiaomi": { "baseUrl": "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1", "api": "openai-completions", "models": [ { "id": "mimo-v2.5", "name": "mimo-v2.5", "api": "openai-completions", "reasoning": false, "input": [ "text", "audio", "video", "image" ], ...... "tools": { "media": { "audio": { "enabled": true, "models": [{ "provider": "xiaomi", "model": "mimo-v2.5" }] }, "video": { "enabled": true, "models": [{ "provider": "xiaomi", "model": "mimo-v2.5" }] } } ``` ## Example

target.title: feat(sessions): auto-revert to primary model after image analysis
target.body:
## Summary

When a session has an auto model override (from media generation fallback) and `revertAfterImageModel` is enabled in config, automatically revert the session model back to the default primary model after image or PDF analysis completes.

This prevents sessions from being "stuck" on a vision-capable fallback model (e.g., GPT-4o) after analyzing images, when the primary model is preferred for follow-up conversation.

## Changes

### New config option
- `agents.defaults.revertAfterImageModel` (boolean, default: false) — When true, auto-revert the session model override back to the primary model after image or PDF analysis completes.

### Modified files
- `src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts` — Add `revertAfterImageModel` type definition
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — Add `revertAfterImageModel` zod schema
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — Add auto-revert logic after image/PDF analysis
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — Add help text for the new config option
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — Add label for the new config option

### Behavior
1. After image/PDF analysis completes, check if the session has an `auto` model override
2. If `revertAfterImageModel` is true, revert the session entry back to the primary model
3. Persist the revert to the session store
4. Log the revert with verbose logging

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Sessions get stuck on vision-capable fallback models (e.g., GPT-4o) after image analysis, even when the primary model (e.g., Claude Sonnet) is preferred for follow-up text conversation. The `revertAfterImageModel` config option was added to automatically revert the model override after image/PDF analysis completes.

**Real environment tested**: openclaw running locally on macOS with Telegram channel, primary model `anthropic/claude-sonnet`, vision fallback `openai/gpt-4o`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```
openclaw config set agents.defaults.revertAfterImageModel true
```
Then sent a JPEG image to the Telegram bot, waited for analysis to complete, and sent a follow-up text message.

**Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture of runtime logs after sending image and follow-up message:
```
[agent-runner] Image analysis completed using openai/gpt-4o (auto override from anthropic/claude-sonnet)
[agent-runner] revertAfterImageModel: reverting session model override from openai/gpt-4o back to primary anthropic/claude-sonnet
[session] Persisted model revert for session agent:main:telegram-208214988
[agent-runner] Running with anthropic/claude-sonnet (primary model restored)
```

**Observed result after fix**: After the image analysis completed, the session model was automatically reverted from `openai/gpt-4o` back to `anthropic/claude-sonnet`. The follow-up text message was processed using the primary model. Session store correctly persisted the revert. Behavior matches the config flag contract — when `revertAfterImageModel` is `false` (default), no revert occurs and the session stays on the fallback model.

**What was not tested**: PDF analysis path (only JPEG tested). Multi-channel setups beyond Telegram. Concurrent image sends during revert.

## Test plan
- [x] Configure `revertAfterImageModel: true` in config
- [x] Send an image to the bot, observe the model falls back to a vision-capable model
- [x] After analysis completes, verify the session reverts to the primary model
- [x] Verify follow-up messages use the primary model

target.title: fix(agents): classify already-at-target edit follower as noop (#60816)
target.body:
### Summary

Reported by @bboguri in #60816: when two identical edits run concurrently against the same file, the leader mutates the file successfully but the follower's `base.execute(...)` then throws `Could not find the exact text in <path>` because the leader already replaced `oldText`. The wrapped recovery layer in `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts:wrapEditToolWithRecovery` had two recovery branches (convert post-write throws to success when the file changed, and append the current file contents to mismatch errors) but no third branch for "the file is unchanged AND already at the requested target state". The follower therefore surfaced as either a misleading error (rethrown mismatch) or, on certain edge inputs, a false success.

clawsweeper-bot's review on this issue prescribed the exact fix shape:

> Add a focused post-state classifier around edit mismatch handling, returning normal success only for writes this request performed, a distinct non-error noop when the requested final state is already satisfied, and preserving errors for true mismatches or write failures.

This PR adds that third branch, gated on the exact-text mismatch error class so unrelated thrown errors keep rethrowing untouched (addresses the [P2] clawsweeper review note on the previous push).

### Fix

`src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts`:

1. New helper `isFileAlreadyInTargetState({ originalContent, currentContent, edits })` returns true only when the originalContent snapshot equals the current file contents AND every edit's newText is present in the file AND every edit's oldText is absent from the file.
2. New helper `buildEditNoopResult(pathParam)` returns the prescribed copy `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.` distinct from the existing `Successfully replaced text in <path>.` used by the post-write recovery branch.
3. `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` consults the noop classifier inside the existing `shouldAddMismatchHint(err)` block, after `didEditLikelyApply` (so genuine post-write throws still report success) and before `appendMismatchHint` (so a false-positive mismatch on a follower no longer re-throws). Any error outside the exact-text mismatch class (aborts, access failures, sandbox bridge errors) keeps rethrowing unchanged.

True mismatches (oldText still present) and write failures (file actually different from the requested state) continue to throw the existing `appendMismatchHint`-decorated error unchanged.

### Tests

`src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-edit-recovery.test.ts`:

- Pin the canonical follower scenario for #60816 (file already in target state, mocked execute throws the exact mismatch, recovery returns the noop result).
- Same contract under the multi-edit `edits[]` shape (two edits, both targets satisfied, both oldText absent).
- Negative pin: true mismatch where newText is present but oldText is also still present (the noop classifier must NOT fire and the mismatch error must rethrow with current file contents).
- New regression for the [P2] clawsweeper review: unrelated thrown error (sandbox bridge timeout) on a file that is already at the target state must rethrow the original error untouched.

### Changelog

Added one bullet under `## Unreleased` -> `### Fixes` in `CHANGELOG.md`, citing #60816 and `Thanks @jeffrey701` per repo convention, noting the gating on the exact-text mismatch error class.

### Note on unrelated CI red

`check-test-types` and `check` are red on this PR head, but every failure is in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-tts.test.ts` (lines 125-198, `'reply' is possibly 'undefined'` and similar). That file was last touched by upstream commit `048a50cfe1 test: tighten auto reply timestamps` on `main` two hours before this push and is not modified by this PR.

Fixes #60816.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Concurrent identical edits on the same file (leader has already replaced `oldText` -> `newText` before the follower's `base.execute(...)` runs). Pre-fix the follower surfaces as either a rethrown `Could not find the exact text in <path>` mismatch or, on edge inputs, a false `Successfully replaced` claim. After this patch the follower is classified as `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.`, and only on the exact-text mismatch error class - sandbox bridge / abort / access errors keep rethrowing untouched.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-23-generic, Node.js v22.22.0, Bash terminal, this PR head `c3b613fdba` checked out. The reproducer below imports `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` directly from `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts` (no mocks of the wrapper itself) and drives it against real files in a real `mkdtemp` directory.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the reproducer below from a fresh shell after checkout of `c3b613fdba`. Command:

    cd /path/to/openclaw
    node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/77638-repro.mts

Reproducer source (`/tmp/77638-repro.mts`), same shape as the four scenarios pinned in the test file, run end-to-end against the wrapper and real `fs`:

    import fs from "node:fs/promises";
    import os from "node:os";
    import path from "node:path";
    import { wrapEditToolWithRecovery } from ".../src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts";

    const tmpDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "openclaw-77638-repro-"));

    function makeTool(opts) {
      const base = { name: "edit", execute: async () => opts.exec() };
      return wrapEditToolWithRecovery(base, {
        root: tmpDir,
        readFile: (p) => fs.readFile(p, "utf-8"),
      });
    }

    // Scenario A: follower already at target - leader replaced "old text" -> "new text"
    // Scenario B: non-mismatch error (sandbox bridge timeout) at target state
    // Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present alongside newText)
    // Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change

Evidence after fix: terminal capture from `node` running the reproducer above, stdout copied live and indented (no leading hash, so it cannot be confused with a markdown heading):

    tmpDir = /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q

    Scenario A: follower already at target -> Edit noop
      isError=false
      text="Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt."
      -> OK

    Scenario B: non-mismatch error at target -> rethrows untouched
      -> THREW: Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run

    Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present) -> hint rethrow
      -> THREW: Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt. The old text must match exactly including all whitespace and newlines.
    Current file contents:
    old text and new text both here

    Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change -> Successfully replaced
      isError=false
      text="Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt."
      -> OK

    repro complete

Observed result after fix: Scenario A (canonical #60816 follower) returned `isError: false` with content text `Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt.` so the new third branch fired and the misleading mismatch and the false `Successfully replaced` are both gone. Scenario B (the [P2] regression) rethrew `Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run` verbatim because the noop classifier did NOT fire (underlying error was not the exact-text mismatch class) even though the file was at the target state. Scenario C rethrew `Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt.` enriched with `Current file contents: old text and new text both here` so the existing `appendMismatchHint` recovery is preserved when oldText is still present. Scenario D returned `Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt.` so the existing `didEditLikelyApply` recovery still fires.

What was not tested: I did not run the full live concurrent-edits race against a running OpenClaw agent talking to the pinned `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.73.0` upstream over `withFileMutationQueue`; the reproducer drives the wrapper directly with throw-shapes that match what the upstream tool emits, mirroring the test fixtures.

target.title: fix(mattermost): include thread files in inbound media
target.body:
## Summary
- collect file IDs from earlier Mattermost thread posts when processing an inbound thread reply
- pass those thread files through the existing inbound media download path so agents can inspect root/reply attachments even when the triggering mention has no files
- dedupe current and repeated file IDs, and fail open if the thread lookup fails

## Related
- Complements #76634, which focuses on resolving Mattermost reply roots before sending thread replies
- Complements #73061, which hydrates thread starter text/placeholders
- Related to #59576, but this fixes the thread-context case where files live on the root or earlier replies rather than the current WebSocket post

## Validation
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-resources.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-resources.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

target.title: fix(ui): format structured tool results instead of "[object Object]
target.body:
## Problem
The Control UI could render structured tool output as `[object Object]` when `toolresult` blocks carried non-string `text` / `content`, or when stream-synthesized messages stored raw object values.
## Change
- Format object-shaped `toolresult.text` and object-shaped non-array `content` with the existing `formatUnknownText(..., { pretty: true })`.
- Normalize streamed tool output through `formatToolOutput(entry.output)`.
- Preserve empty-string outputs by avoiding truthy checks that drop `""`.
## Tests
- Added coverage for object-shaped `toolresult.text`.
- Added coverage for object-shaped stream results.
- Verified synthesized message content contains stringified JSON, not `[object Object]`.
## Verification
- `cd ui && npm run build` passed.
- `cd ui && npm test` ran unit tests successfully, but the full command exited non-zero because Playwright Chromium is not installed in this local environment.
## Local test note
Vitest’s Playwright project could not launch Chromium from `~/.cache/ms-playwright`. This appears to be an environment issue, not a failure in the updated code or assertions.
## Maintainer note
The empty-string behavior changed intentionally from truthy omission to explicit preservation. If preferred, this can be adjusted to hide empty outputs while still fixing object formatting.
