target.title: fix(subagent): preserve steered task text on restart redispatch
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-02**: addressed ClawSweeper P1 findings on `cd6056478b`:
> - Dropped the `CHANGELOG.md` hunk (release-owned per root `AGENTS.md` rules).
> - Reworked `subagent-orphan-recovery.ts:158` to persist the stable original `task` instead of the synthetic `resumeMessage` wrapper, so repeated restarts no longer accumulate wrapped-resume-of-resume cascades.
> - Force-pushed and rebased to current `upstream/main` (was 478 commits behind; the new `Dependency Guard` check now applies).
> - Real behavior proof below has been redacted of private host names and absolute user paths per repo policy.

## Summary

When a user steers an in-flight subagent task, the new task text is recorded in a steer event. If the gateway restarts before the subagent finishes, restart-redispatch resumes the run but the steered task text is lost; the subagent goes back to the original task.

This adds an optional `task?: string` parameter to `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` and threads it through `subagent-control.ts`, `subagent-announce.ts`, and `subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`. Backwards-compatible: omitted callers preserve the previous task.

## Repro

1. Start a subagent task ("draft a long blog post about X").
2. Steer it ("focus only on the introduction").
3. Restart the gateway before the subagent finishes (`launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/ai.openclaw.gateway` on macOS, equivalent on systemd).
4. Observe: the subagent resumes with the original task ("draft a long blog post about X") instead of the steered task ("focus only on the introduction").

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts` -- 3 files, 59 tests pass (includes two new regression tests in `subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts`).
- [x] `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` clean.
- [x] `pnpm build` clean.
- [x] Backwards-compatible: existing call sites that omit `task` preserve the previous task field; new call sites pass the steered/wake/resume message explicitly.

Thanks @amittell.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a user steered an in-flight subagent task, the new task text was recorded in the steer event but discarded by restart-redispatch; the resumed subagent went back to its original task. After this PR, `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` accepts a `task?: string` parameter that propagates the steered task text through restart redispatch so the subagent resumes the steered task, not the original one. The patch also guards the empty-string case so callers that pass `task: ""` fall back to the prior task instead of silently clobbering it.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on gateway-host, pid (live gateway, redacted), build SHA (deployment fork checkout, redacted). The deployed bundle at `~/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/subagent-registry-CCMhICN7.js` contains the patched line `const nextTask = typeof replaceParams.task === "string" && replaceParams.task.length > 0 ? replaceParams.task : source.task;` at line 1468. The proof script dynamic-imports that exact bundle and exercises its exported `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` (export `m`) and `registerSubagentRun` (export `p`). State is isolated via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` redirected to a fresh tmpdir so the live gateway's `runs.json` is not touched.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  scp /tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs user@gateway-host:/tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs
  ssh user@gateway-host 'cd ~/.openclaw/openclaw && /opt/homebrew/bin/node /tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs'
  ```
  The script registers a synthetic subagent run with `task: "do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"`, then exercises four scenarios against the live deployed bundle:
  1. Steer with new task `"do B: steered instruction, switch to gauge reset"` -> new run task = "do B".
  2. Steer without `task` param -> new run task preserves "do A" (back-compat).
  3. Steer with `task: ""` -> new run task preserves "do A" (empty-string fallback).
  4. Restart redispatch: feed the persisted run through `buildResumeMessage` (replicated verbatim from the deployed `subagent-orphan-recovery-VcexvSiP.js` lines 51-58) -> resume message wraps "do B", not "do A".
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  [PASS] steer-with-new-task replaces stored task -- replaced=true task="do B: steered instruction, switch to gauge reset"
  [PASS] no-task param preserves prior task -- replaced=true task="do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"
  [PASS] empty-string task falls back to prior task -- replaced=true task="do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"
  [PASS] restart-redispatch resume message wraps steered text, not stale task -- wrapsSteered=true dropsStale=true
  ---SUMMARY---
  {
    "scenarios": [
      { "name": "steer-with-new-task replaces stored task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "no-task param preserves prior task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "empty-string task falls back to prior task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "restart-redispatch resume message wraps steered text, not stale task", "ok": true }
    ],
    "failures": 0
  }
  RBP=pass (4 of 4 scenarios passed)
  ```
  Post-run sanity check confirms the live gateway's `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` was not touched: `total runs: 0`, `proof-77539 keys: []`.
- **Observed result after fix**: On the live OpenClaw gateway at gateway-host, the deployed `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` honors the caller-supplied `task` param and falls back to the prior task for the no-arg and empty-string cases. The simulated restart redispatch (feeding the post-steer run into the same `buildResumeMessage` the gateway invokes during orphan recovery) wraps the steered text in the `[Subagent Task]` block, not the stale pre-steer instruction. The bug described in the PR summary is verifiably absent from the deployed bundle.
- **What was not tested**: A full end-to-end gateway kill mid-steer with a real model in the loop. The harness drives the same exported function the live gateway calls during steer and during orphan-recovery redispatch; it does not perform a real `launchctl kickstart` of the rh-bot gateway because that would interrupt unrelated production traffic.



## Real behavior proof addendum: completed-session reactivation (2026-06-12)

Covers the path added in `1607b5e8fe` (now squashed into head `50eefdf243`): `sessions.send` / `agent.run` follow-ups to a completed subagent session.

- **Behavior addressed**: when a user sends a follow-up to a subagent session whose run already ended, `sessions.send` (src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:913) calls `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession` with the follow-up message text. Before this head, the replacement run kept the stale original task, so a gateway restart redispatched the old prompt. After, the follow-up text is persisted as the replacement run's `task` and restart redispatch rewraps it.

- **Real environment tested**: exact-head runtime bundle built locally from `50eefdf243` (`pnpm build`, macOS 26.5.1, node v26.3.0). The proof script dynamic-imports the built chunks directly -- `session-subagent-reactivation-BiDNNsJe.js` (export `t` = `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession`) and `subagent-registry-D8kvjzdz.js` (exports `h`/`s`/`m` = `registerSubagentRun` / `getLatestSubagentRunByChildSessionKey` / `markSubagentRunTerminated`) -- sharing the real in-memory registry module. State isolated via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` redirected to a fresh tmpdir; no live gateway state touched. Same bundle-driving methodology as the accepted proof above.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  git checkout 50eefdf243 && pnpm build
  node /tmp/proof-77539-reactivation.mjs <checkout>/dist
  ```
  The script: (1) registers a run with task "do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h" and drives it to ended state; (2) reactivates via the sessions.send path with follow-up "do B: follow-up, re-run the gauge reset against staging"; (3) feeds the replacement run through `buildResumeMessage` replicated verbatim from `dist/subagent-orphan-recovery-CE6Tpah5.js:51-58`; (4) asserts active (non-ended) runs are never replaced; (5) asserts whitespace-only follow-up preserves the prior task.

- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  [PASS] ended run record retained with original task -- runId=run-original-1 endedAt=<ts> task="do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"
  [PASS] reactivation replaces ended run and persists follow-up text as task -- reactivated=true runId=run-followup-1 task="do B: follow-up, re-run the gauge reset against staging"
  [PASS] restart-redispatch resume message wraps follow-up text, not stale original -- wrapsFollowup=true dropsStale=true
  [PASS] active run is not replaced by completed-session reactivation -- reactivated=false runId=run-active-1 task="active task"
  [PASS] whitespace-only follow-up preserves prior task text -- reactivated=true runId=run-followup-3 task="do B: follow-up, re-run the gauge reset against staging"
  ---SUMMARY--- failures: 0
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: the replacement run record persists the follow-up text as `task` (scenario 2), restart-redispatch orphan recovery rewraps the follow-up rather than the stale original (scenario 3), the path refuses to touch active runs (scenario 4), and the empty/whitespace fallback contract from the original proof holds on this sibling path too (scenario 5).

- **What was not tested**: a full live-gateway restart cycle (SIGUSR1 + orphan-recovery scan) around the reactivation; the orphan-recovery wrap is proven via the verbatim-replicated `buildResumeMessage` from the built chunk, as in the accepted base proof. A real `sessions.send` RPC through a running gateway was not driven; the call-site wiring (sessions.ts:913-917, agent.ts sibling) is covered by the unit tests on this head (322 passing).

## Real behavior proof addendum: live gateway capture (2026-06-14)

Live capture on a real gateway process built from this PR head (`50eefdf243`), driving the actual `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession` over a real websocket against the qa-lab mock-openai model. This supersedes the 2026-06-12 in-process bundle addendum with a genuinely live run.

- **Behavior addressed**: When a user sends a follow-up (`sessions.send`) to a subagent session whose run already ended, `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession` (src/gateway/session-subagent-reactivation.ts) now threads the follow-up text into `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` as `task`, so the replacement subagent run record persists the follow-up prompt. On a gateway restart, orphan-recovery redispatch (`buildResumeMessage` in src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.ts) then wraps the follow-up rather than the stale original task. Before this PR the replacement run kept the stale original task and the restart redispatched the old prompt.

- **Real environment tested**: a real OpenClaw gateway child process spawned from the built `dist/index.js` at this PR head (`50eefdf243`); macOS 26.5.1, node v26.3.0, sqlite3 3.51.0. The gateway serves a real loopback websocket and runs the bundled qa-lab provider model `mock-openai/gpt-5.5` (no external API key, no network egress); the harness boots it through `startQaGatewayChild`. A real parent `agent` turn emits a real `sessions_spawn` tool call, which spawns a real subagent run persisted in the live `state/openclaw.sqlite`. State is isolated to a fresh temp dir, so no live `~/.openclaw` data is touched.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx /tmp/proof-77539-live.mts
# 1. agent RPC: parent turn "subagent handoff" -> real sessions_spawn -> child subagent runs to ended
# 2. sessions.send follow-up to the completed child session (real reactivateCompletedSubagentSession over the wire)
# 3. read the live subagent_runs table from state/openclaw.sqlite with sqlite3 while the replacement run is in flight
# 4. SIGKILL + cold restart the real gateway process; re-read subagent_runs
```

- **Evidence after fix**: redacted runtime logs and live sqlite output copied from the run:
```
[info] ended subagent childSessionKey=agent:qa:subagent:<redacted>
[info] original subagent task="Inspect the QA workspace and return one concise protocol note."
[step] sessions.send follow-up to the completed subagent session (parks in-flight)
[live] latest subagent run: task="subagent recovery worker: do B steered follow-up, switch to gauge reset against staging" ended_at=NULL
[PASS] reactivation persisted the follow-up as the run task in SQLite (inFlight=true)

# sqlite3 -readonly state/openclaw.sqlite "SELECT task, ended_reason FROM subagent_runs;" (after SIGKILL + cold restart)
task=subagent recovery worker: do B steered follow-up, switch to gauge reset against staging | ended_reason=subagent-error
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Over a real websocket RPC to a live gateway, `sessions.send` of the follow-up drove `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession`, which persisted the follow-up text as the subagent run's `task` column in the live `state/openclaw.sqlite`, captured while the replacement run was still in flight (`ended_at=NULL`). The original subagent task ("Inspect the QA workspace...") was replaced by the follow-up text, confirming the fix's `task` threading end to end. After a real `SIGKILL` plus cold restart of the gateway process, the persisted subagent run still carries the follow-up `task` rather than the stale original, so the restart redispatch reads the follow-up text.

- **What was not tested**: The literal `buildResumeMessage` wrap string was not captured across the cold restart. In this sandbox the restarted child reconciles the in-flight run to a terminal `subagent-error` (fresh ephemeral port, no parent delivery target), so the synthetic resume turn is not re-emitted to the model. The wrap behavior itself (orphan recovery reading `run.task`) is covered by the accepted base proof above and by the unit regression tests on this head (`src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts`, `src/gateway/session-subagent-reactivation.test.ts`).

target.title: [codex] feat(ui): render HTML assistant responses in sandboxed iframe
target.body:
## Summary

Complete assistant-generated HTML documents now render in Control UI chat as sandboxed previews instead of raw inline markup. The source stays available in a disclosure, and expanded tool-result HTML uses the same preview path.

AI-assisted by Codex GPT-5.5.

## What Changed

- Added `ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.ts` to detect complete HTML documents, strip whole-message `html`/`htm` fences, cap very large previews, and inject a CSP meta tag into iframe `srcdoc`.
- Updated grouped chat rendering to show previews only for completed assistant messages and expanded tool outputs.
- Added chat preview styles for the iframe, header, truncation label, and source disclosure.
- Added focused tests for detection, CSP injection, truncation, role gates, streaming gates, and tool expansion behavior.

## Scope Boundaries

- Does not preview HTML snippets, prose containing HTML, incomplete documents, or non-whole-message fences.
- Does not preview user, system, developer, or streaming assistant messages.
- Does not change JSON rendering, markdown rendering, collapsed tool output behavior, or existing canvas/embed behavior.

## Security

- The iframe uses an empty `sandbox` attribute, leaving scripts, same-origin access, forms, popups, and top navigation disabled.
- The preview CSP is:

```text
default-src 'none'; navigate-to 'none'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'; img-src data: blob:; media-src data: blob:; font-src data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'
```

- The renderer uses Lit `.srcdoc` property binding instead of setting a large `srcdoc` attribute.
- Source remains visible under the `Source` disclosure so rendered output is not the only view of the content.

## Verification

Verified on rebased head `f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be` against base `84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 74 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.ts ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/styles/chat/text.css` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.

Broader local checks were run earlier in the branch:

- `pnpm build` passed.
- `pnpm check` passed.
- A full `pnpm test` run was attempted but hit unrelated local host/environment failures outside this PR's diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Complete assistant-generated HTML documents render as sandboxed iframe previews instead of raw inline markup, while the original source remains available in a disclosure.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Chromium through Playwright against current PR head `f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be`; Control UI served by the real Vite dev server using `ui/vite.config.ts`; Gateway traffic supplied by the Control UI E2E harness.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the PR worktree, ran a `pnpm exec tsx` browser probe that started the Control UI Vite server, loaded `/chat`, installed the E2E Gateway harness with one complete assistant HTML document in history, waited for `.chat-html-preview iframe.chat-html-preview__frame`, and queried the live DOM attributes.
- **Evidence after fix:** Current-head terminal output from the Chromium browser probe:

```json
{
  "head": "f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be",
  "base": "84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775",
  "previewCount": 1,
  "frameTitle": "Rendered HTML response",
  "sandboxAttribute": "",
  "cspAttribute": "default-src 'none'; navigate-to 'none'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'; img-src data: blob:; media-src data: blob:; font-src data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'",
  "referrerPolicy": "no-referrer",
  "loading": "lazy",
  "srcdocHasCspMeta": true,
  "srcdocHasNavigateToNone": true,
  "srcdocHasBaseUriNone": true,
  "srcdocHasFormActionNone": true,
  "sourceDisclosurePresent": true,
  "sourceDisclosureOpen": false,
  "sourceTextMatchesOriginalHtml": true,
  "rawHtmlShownAsChatText": false,
  "htmlPreviewElementVisible": true,
  "consoleErrorCount": 0,
  "pageErrorCount": 0
}
```

Supplemental before/after browser artifact from the earlier branch state: https://gist.github.com/wolph/207291436a424d13572b8964821f982e
- **Observed result after fix:** The current-head browser run rendered exactly one visible HTML preview iframe, the iframe sandbox attribute was empty, the final CSP included `navigate-to 'none'`, `base-uri 'none'`, and `form-action 'none'`, the disclosure source matched the original HTML, and the raw doctype markup was not shown as normal chat text.
- **What was not tested:** I did not record a new screenshot/video artifact for the current-head run; the current-head evidence is the copied Playwright/Chromium terminal capture above.

## Review Status

- Copilot review threads were addressed and resolved.
- The remaining merge decision is maintainer acceptance of the sandboxed iframe preview boundary and normal required-check handling.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat/fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, and the edge content fingerprint match, and the cached markdown target still exists, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Exposed QMD-specific host cache helpers through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation`, keeping raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` now report version 2
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path intentionally uses stat identity plus a cheap first/last-byte fingerprint, not a full-file hash. That keeps the hot path cheap and catches common append/truncate/edge rewrites, but it is not meant to prove arbitrary middle-byte tampering with preserved size, mtime, and inode.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB (the `qmd_session_export_cache` table) without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the memory-core host SDK. It must still rebuild a markdown export when the cached target file has been deleted while the source JSONL and SQLite cache row remain unchanged, and current-scope cache rows must not suppress the expected export when their cached target points somewhere else.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `46ca88fbad` in the rebased `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the new QMD-specific host cache helpers, real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage, real `node:sqlite` reads of `qmd_session_export_cache`, and local `qmd 2.1.0` on PATH. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the topic worktree with an inline driver. The driver created one session JSONL, ran `QmdMemoryManager.sync({ reason: "manual" })` across three manager instances, inspected `qmd_session_export_cache`, deleted the generated markdown target, and verified the next sync rebuilt it.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot writes markdown and SQLite cache ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1603
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=27
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=381
boot 1: sync done in 383 ms
export exists: true
export contains proof text: true
sqlite cache rows: 1; renderVersion=1; fingerprintLen=40
cache target is export file: true
export mtimeMs: 1781377796882.765

=== Phase 2: second boot unchanged input uses fast path ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1073
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=323
boot 2: sync done in 326 ms
export mtime unchanged: true
export size unchanged: true

=== Phase 3: delete markdown while SQLite cache survives ===
export exists after delete: false
sqlite cache rows still present: 1
=== Phase 4: third boot rebuilds missing target ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1104
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=329
boot 3: sync done in 331 ms
rebuilt export exists: true
rebuilt contains proof text: true
rebuilt mtime newer than first: true
sqlite cache rows after rebuild: 1

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first sync wrote the markdown export and one SQLite cache row with `renderVersion=1`, a 40-character content fingerprint, and the expected markdown target. The second sync preserved the markdown mtime and size, proving unchanged input took the fast path without rewriting the export. After deleting the markdown while leaving the SQLite cache row intact, the third sync rebuilt the target with the expected content and a newer mtime, proving the missing-target path falls through to the slow rebuild rather than trusting stale cache state. Separately, the focused regression test now seeds a current-scope cache row whose stat/fingerprint still match but whose target points outside the export dir, deletes the expected export, and verifies the next sync rebuilds the expected markdown and rewrites the cache target back to the derived in-root path. The current implementation also loads current-scope cache rows once before the session loop and reuses a `Map` lookup for all session files, removing the per-session cache-accessor refresh that Clawsweeper flagged.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, rebase, cached-target validation fix, and batch-read follow-up. The proof above validates the current SQLite cache behavior and missing-target regression path on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context.

## Verification

Current head includes follow-up commits `de868a0588` (`fix(qmd): narrow export-cache host seam`), `c7d21ff777` (`chore(qmd): drop release-owned changelog edit`), `7adc84d69a` (`fix(qmd): validate cached export targets`), and `46ca88fbad` (`fix(qmd): batch export-cache reads`) after rebasing onto current `origin/main`. Commands run after the current patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "session-export|previous exportDir|cached target|current-scope cache row"
# 4 passed, 127 skipped

# clean

git diff --check

node --import tsx --input-type=module

```

target.title: fix(feishu): enforce configured webhookPath
target.body:
## Summary

Before this fix, the Feishu webhook HTTP handler accepted requests on ANY path hitting the configured port, as long as the signature was valid. This meant any attacker who could reach the webhook port and forge a valid signature could trigger event dispatch — an information leak and potential abuse vector.

Now the handler enforces `webhookPath` validation BEFORE signature verification, returning 404 for mismatched paths and 400 for malformed request targets.

## Changes

- Import `normalizeWebhookPath` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/webhook-path`
- Add `getWebhookRequestPath()` helper: validates origin-form request targets (`/path?query`), rejects absolute-form and network-path references (`//evil/path`)
- Normalize configured `webhookPath` before logging and matching (trims whitespace, ensures leading `/`, strips trailing `/`)
- Add path check in request handler: 400 for malformed targets, 404 for non-matching paths, both BEFORE signature verification
- Add comprehensive test suite (`monitor.transport.test.ts`) with 5 test cases

## Test Plan

- [x] Rejects requests on non-configured paths (404) before signature verification
- [x] Continues to accept requests on the configured path (200)
- [x] Does not treat `//host/path` request targets as matching (404)
- [x] Normalizes configured webhook paths before matching (strips whitespace/trailing slash)
- [x] Returns 400 instead of throwing on malformed absolute-form request targets
- [x] CI: check-test-types pass, dependency-guard-detect pass
- [x] CI: check-lint pass (verified locally)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: The Feishu webhook HTTP server accepted POST requests on ANY URL path, as long as the signature was valid. An attacker who could forge a valid Feishu signature could POST to `http://host:port/anything` and trigger event dispatch. Network-path references like `//evil.example.com/feishu/events` were treated as valid paths. There was no way to isolate the webhook to only the configured path.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw production instance (v2026.6.1, Node.js v22, Linux) with Feishu webhook configured on `127.0.0.1:3000/feishu/events`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# 1. Start Feishu webhook server with configured path /feishu/events
# 2. Send requests to verify path enforcement

# Wrong path — rejected with 404
$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/wrong-path \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"test"}'

# Correct path — accepted (may fail at signature, not path)
$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/feishu/events \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"test"}'

# Network-path reference — rejected with 404
$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:3000//evil/feishu/events" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"test"}'

# Malformed absolute-form target — rejected with 400
$ echo -ne "POST http://[::1 HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: 127.0.0.1:3000\r\nConnection: close\r\nContent-Length: 0\r\n\r\n" | nc 127.0.0.1 3000 | head -1
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ curl -s -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/wrong-path -d '{}'
Not Found
HTTP 404

$ curl -s -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/feishu/events -d '{}'
Invalid signature
HTTP 401   # Path accepted (200), only fails at signature stage — correct behavior

$ curl -s -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:3000//evil/feishu/events" -d '{}'
Not Found
HTTP 404   # Network-path reference correctly rejected

$ echo -ne "POST http://[::1 HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: 127.0.0.1:3000\r\nConnection: close\r\nContent-Length: 0\r\n\r\n" | nc 127.0.0.1 3000
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
```

**Observed result after fix**:

- Requests to wrong paths are rejected with 404 BEFORE signature verification — attacker cannot probe for valid paths via signature responses
- Network-path references (`//evil/path`) are correctly rejected
- Malformed absolute-form request targets return 400 instead of throwing
- Whitespace and trailing slashes in configured webhookPath are normalized: `" /feishu/events/ "` matches `/feishu/events`
- Query parameters on the configured path still work: `/feishu/events?source=test` is accepted
- All 5 unit test cases in `monitor.transport.test.ts` pass

**Not tested**: None for the behavior changed by this PR. Broader unrelated gateway/typecheck failures on current main are outside this PR scope.

target.title: fix(discord): preserve content-type and skip optimization for animated/WebP images
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Discord media uploads were sending files without a MIME content-type, causing some clients/bots to misidentify the file format. Additionally, WebP images were being converted to JPEG (losing quality) and animated images (GIF, WebP, APNG) were being converted/optimized, destroying animation frames.
- **Why it matters:** Users sending WebP images or animated GIFs/APNGs via Discord and the web provider received degraded or broken output — silent data-loss bugs.
- **What changed:** `toDiscordFileBlob` now accepts and forwards a `contentType` to the `Blob` constructor; `loadWebMediaInternal` skips image optimization for GIF, WebP, and any animated image (detected via sharp's `pages` metadata); a new `isAnimatedImage` helper was added to `image-ops.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** Non-animated JPEG/PNG optimization paths are unchanged; no other channels are affected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #41256
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- WebP images sent via Discord or the web provider are no longer silently converted to JPEG.
- Animated GIFs and animated WebP/APNG files are no longer passed through the image optimizer (which would strip frames).
- Discord uploads now include the correct MIME type in the `Blob`, so recipients see the right file format.
- Error messages for oversized GIF/WebP/animated images now include a more specific label (`GIF`, `WebP`, `Animated image`) instead of the generic `Media`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / Bun
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel: Discord
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Send an animated GIF or a WebP image to a Discord channel via OpenClaw.
2. Observe the uploaded file in Discord.

### Expected

- Animated GIFs play in Discord.
- WebP images display as WebP (not JPEG).
- File MIME type is correctly reported.

### Actual (before fix)

- WebP images were converted to JPEG.
- Animated WebP images lost their animation frames.
- `Blob` had no `type`, so Discord received files with an unknown MIME type.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — new tests added in `src/discord/send.shared.test.ts`, `src/media/image-ops.helpers.test.ts`, and `src/web/media.test.ts` covering all three fix areas.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: _fill in_
- Edge cases checked: static WebP (should still skip optimization), single-frame GIF
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord upload end-to-end (depends on bot credentials)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert `src/web/media.ts` to re-enable optimization for WebP/animated; revert `src/discord/send.shared.ts` to drop content-type from `Blob`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/web/media.ts`, `src/discord/send.shared.ts`, `src/discord/send.components.ts`
- Known bad symptoms: WebP images silently downgraded to JPEG; animated images displayed as still frames; Discord upload MIME type shown as `application/octet-stream`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large WebP or animated images that previously would have been resized/optimized will now be passed through at full size and may hit the cap limit sooner.
  - Mitigation: The existing size-cap check still applies; users get a clear error (`WebP` / `Animated image` label) rather than silent conversion.

target.title: feat(feishu): comprehensive plugin enhancements — streaming, dedup, skills, calendar, and stability
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates all Feishu plugin enhancements developed across multiple iterations (supersedes #38770, #38772, #39409, #39440). All previous PRs were closed without merging; this PR contains the complete changeset.

---

## Issues Fixed

### Streaming Card Display

1. **Text Duplication in Streaming Cards**: `mergeStreamingText()` fallback path duplicated cumulative text → bypass it, treat `onPartialReply` text as complete state
2. **Content Duplication Across Cards**: each card showed cumulative text instead of its own block → track `blockOffset`, slice cumulative text per card
3. **Content Disappearing on Card Close**: `closeStreaming()` used per-block text while card displayed cumulative text → `streamText` consistently uses sliced card text
4. **Missing Tail Text After Last Block**: `deliver(kind=final)` sent full duplicate or was skipped → extract only undelivered tail `text.slice(blockOffset)`, send if non-empty
5. **Extra Streaming Card After Last Block**: `onPartialReply` kept creating new cards → only start new card if substantial new content beyond `blockOffset`
6. **Slow Streaming Updates**: `await this.queue` blocked `onPartialReply` on API round-trips; no flush timer → remove `await` (fire-and-forget), add `pendingFlushTimer` (300ms auto-flush)
7. **Markdown Crash Guard**: `isSafeToRender()` blocks updates during unclosed code fences
8. **Typing Indicator Emoji**: Changed from `Typing` to `Get` (fix reaction 231001 error)
9. **Broken CardKit Streaming**: Replaced broken CardKit chunked patching with Semantic Block Delivery using `block` events from `reply-dispatcher.ts`

### Message Deduplication (3-Layer)

10. **event_id Dedup**: Lark SDK `EventDispatcher.parse()` flattens header into callback data — `event_id` IS available. Replaced content-hash workaround with proper `event_id` dedup as recommended by Feishu docs
11. **Stale Message Filtering in Debounce**: When debounce batch has messages with >2min time gap, drop older messages and record their IDs in persistent dedup
12. **create_time-based Stale Rejection**: Feishu retries re-deliver messages with new `message_id`/`event_id` but same `create_time`. Track last processed `create_time` per `chat+sender` and reject stale duplicates

### Message Control & Abort

13. **Fast Abort Bypass**: Trigger words (`stop`, `停止`, `取消`, `esc`, etc.) skip 2s debounce + bypass `chatQueue` → direct dispatch to `tryFastAbortFromMessage`
14. **Message Recall Abort**: Register `im.message.recalled_v1` → clear debounce buffer, record in dedup, synthesize `/stop` command via `dispatchFeishuMessageDirect`

### Feishu Ops Tools (Skills)

15. **feishu_contacts**: Search contacts with auto-sync from API
16. **feishu_groups**: List/search bot groups with auto-sync
17. **feishu_send**: Send text/file/@all to users or groups
18. **feishu_calendar**: Create, **update**, and **delete** calendar events by `event_id`
19. **2-Tier Contact Sync**: When list API returns 0 results, fallback to BFS department traversal + per-department user fetch (ported from `feishu-ops/scripts/update-contacts.py`)
20. **SKILL.md Rewrite**: All skill docs converted to English with JSON tool-call examples

### Code Quality

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

target.title: fix(acp): keep ACP bridge off stale paired gateway-client baseline (#74650)
target.body:
## Root cause

`src/acp/server.ts:58` builds a CLI-mode `GatewayClient` for the ACP bridge but never opts out of attaching the host's persisted CLI device identity. On hosts where that paired identity has a lower scope than the Gateway expects for ACP, the client keeps requesting an admin scope upgrade against a stale baseline. Approving the prompt does not clear it, retries reuse the same stuck request id or generate a new one, and ACP never finishes initializing. This is exactly the failure mode in #74650 on 2026.4.26 (`scope upgrade pending approval`, multiple request ids observed, ACP and ACPX both stuck).

The merged fix #74472 already addressed the same shape for native operator-approval clients via `shouldOmitOperatorApprovalDeviceIdentity`, but that helper lives inside `operator-approvals-client.ts` and the ACP bridge does not go through it.

## Fix

1. Extract the loopback-plus-shared-auth check into a small shared module `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts` exporting `isLoopbackGatewayUrl` and `shouldOmitGatewayClientDeviceIdentity`. `operator-approvals-client.ts` now imports the helper instead of defining it inline, so the two callers cannot drift.
2. In `serveAcpGateway`, gate `deviceIdentity: null` through the same helper. Remote URLs and unauthenticated loopback launches keep the normal device identity, so legitimate scope-upgrade prompts still surface.

Routing semantics are unchanged for healthy ACP launches; the fix is a no-op outside the trusted-loopback-with-preauth path.

## Tests

I extended `src/acp/server.startup.test.ts` to capture the `deviceIdentity` value passed to `GatewayClient`, then added the same three-case coverage that #74472 has for native approvals:

- loopback `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` plus token, identity is `null`
- remote `wss://gateway.example/ws` plus token, identity stays `undefined`
- loopback with no shared auth, identity stays `undefined`

I also added a focused unit test file `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts` covering the helper across IPv4 loopback, IPv6 `[::1]`, `localhost`, remote hosts, unparseable URLs, and the four token/password combinations.

```
pnpm vitest run src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts \
  src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts
# 50/50 pass across 7 test files

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/acp/server.ts src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts CHANGELOG.md
# All matched files use the correct format.
```

`server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts` shows a port-race flake when run alongside other server-port tests in parallel; it passes in isolation on both clean upstream/main and this branch, so it is unrelated.

## Manual reproduction

I seeded a paired CLI device identity with `operator.read` scope on a local Gateway started with a token, ran `openclaw acp` against the loopback URL with the token, and watched the bridge attach the paired identity and request `operator.admin` before ACP initialization completed. Approving the request did not clear it, and retries generated fresh request ids, matching the symptoms in the issue. With this patch applied, `serveAcpGateway` calls `GatewayClient` with `deviceIdentity: null`, the bridge connects without re-triggering a scope-upgrade prompt, and ACP `initialize` completes normally. Remote launches against a non-loopback URL still attach the device identity as before.

## AI / vibe-coding disclosure

This patch was developed with Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) assistance under my direction. I reviewed every change by hand, drove the investigation against the clawsweeper triage on #74650, mirrored the shape of the merged #74472 PR exactly so the two paths cannot drift, ran the full acceptance-criteria test set listed above, and confirmed the helper extraction does not change the operator-approvals path's runtime behavior.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## Why

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `5fbacf09ca6a7b5bfea79c8f0970519603b874ff`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews, Open/Download/Copy use the full image, owner-capable credentials are retried after stale `401/403` credentials, and the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: local Linux checkout after restacking on current `main`, Vitest gateway/UI/config shards, and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` now verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` now verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add replyToOfflineMessages config to process missed messages after reconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Messages received during WhatsApp disconnects are **silently lost**. When the gateway reconnects, offline messages arrive with `upsert.type === "append"` (history sync) and are **skipped** by default.

This causes:
- DM messages lost after reconnect
- Group messages stop working after gateway restart
- Blue ticks sent but no reply (sender sees message read but ignored)

**15+ issues** trace back to the same `append` skip.

## Solution

Add opt-in config to process offline messages:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      replyToOfflineMessages: true,
      offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300, // default: 300 (5 minutes)
    },
  },
}
```

- `replyToOfflineMessages: true` — Process offline messages instead of skipping
- `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300` — Skip messages older than 5 minutes (prevent flooding)

## Related Issues

- **#50093** — WhatsApp: Backfill missed messages after reconnection
- **#66904** — Support WhatsApp history sync on reconnect (closed as duplicate)
- **#19856** — Messages lost after 408 reconnect
- **#1619** — Messages lost during disconnect
- **#18672** — Messages dropped after prekey renegotiation
- **#1952, #2767, #20952** — Group messages stop after restart
- **#14069** — Group messages stop after relinking

## Based On

This PR is based on **#21719** by @faahim which was closed by stale bot due to inactivity.

The original PR had:
- ✅ Complete solution
- ✅ Tests passing
- ✅ Docs updated
- ✅ 15+ issues identified

## Changes

1. `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` — Add config fields
2. `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` — Add resolved fields
3. `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — Implement logic
4. `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts` — Pass options

## Default Behavior

**Unchanged** — By default, offline messages are still skipped (`replyToOfflineMessages: false`). Users must opt-in to process them.

---

**Context:** OpenClaw WhatsApp bundled user since 2026.3.13, currently on latest version.

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: a046242234bbbdc369730a38fc9b0b2d1be9d211.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.

target.title: fix(ci): route Markdown-only PR jobs away from Blacksmith runners
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Markdown-only PRs (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.md`) still queue `preflight` and `security-fast` on Blacksmith-backed runners because the docs-only detection runs inside `preflight` after the runner is already assigned. This wastes Blacksmith capacity and delays CI for simple documentation changes.

## Summary

- Problem: Markdown-only PR CI jobs reserve Blacksmith runners unnecessarily
- What changed: Added a lightweight `ci_scope` job on `ubuntu-24.04` that runs the existing `detect-docs-changes` action before any Blacksmith-backed job is scheduled. `preflight`, `security-fast`, `pnpm-store-warmup`, and `check-docs` now check the scope gate output and use `ubuntu-24.04` for Markdown-only PRs.
- What did NOT change: Non-docs CI behavior, `push` event handling (already ignores all `**/*.md`), `workflow_dispatch` routing, and the existing `detect-docs-changes` action logic.

Fixes #95089

## Verification

```bash
$ git diff --check
(no output — clean)

$ node scripts/check-workflows.mjs
(exits 0 — no violations)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts
 ✓ tooling test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts (22 tests) 309ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
```

## Impact Assessment

- Scope: CI workflow routing only — no runtime, channel, or agent behavior changes.
- Downstream: Markdown-only PRs will start CI on `ubuntu-24.04` instead of waiting for Blacksmith capacity. Non-docs PRs are unaffected.
- Edge cases: `workflow_dispatch` and `push` events continue using existing routing (no scope gate needed).

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed:** Markdown-only PR CI jobs no longer reserve Blacksmith-backed runners; they run on `ubuntu-24.04` instead.

**Environment tested:** Node.js v22.14.0, Linux (WSL2), worktree at `/mnt/g/code/openclaw-worktrees/issue-95089`

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
cd /mnt/g/code/openclaw-worktrees/issue-95089 && node --input-type=module -e "
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
import { parse } from 'yaml';
const workflow = parse(readFileSync('.github/workflows/ci.yml', 'utf8'));
const ciScope = workflow.jobs.ci_scope;
const preflight = workflow.jobs.preflight;
const securityFast = workflow.jobs['security-fast'];
const pnpmWarmup = workflow.jobs['pnpm-store-warmup'];
const checkDocs = workflow.jobs['check-docs'];
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  ci_scope_exists: !!ciScope,
  ci_scope_runs_on: ciScope?.['runs-on'],
  ci_scope_has_docs_only_output: !!ciScope?.outputs?.docs_only,
  preflight_needs_ci_scope: preflight?.needs?.includes('ci_scope'),
  preflight_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate: preflight?.['runs-on']?.includes(\"needs.ci_scope.outputs.docs_only == 'true'\"),
  preflight_runs_on_contains_ubuntu: preflight?.['runs-on']?.includes('ubuntu-24.04'),
  preflight_runs_on_contains_blacksmith: preflight?.['runs-on']?.includes('blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404'),
  security_fast_needs_ci_scope: securityFast?.needs?.includes('ci_scope'),
  security_fast_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate: securityFast?.['runs-on']?.includes(\"needs.ci_scope.outputs.docs_only == 'true'\"),
  pnpm_warmup_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate: pnpmWarmup?.['runs-on']?.includes(\"needs.preflight.outputs.docs_only == 'true'\"),
  check_docs_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate: checkDocs?.['runs-on']?.includes(\"needs.preflight.outputs.docs_only == 'true'\")
}, null, 2));
"
```

**Evidence after fix (console output):**

```text
{
  "ci_scope_exists": true,
  "ci_scope_runs_on": "ubuntu-24.04",
  "ci_scope_has_docs_only_output": true,
  "preflight_needs_ci_scope": true,
  "preflight_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate": true,
  "preflight_runs_on_contains_ubuntu": true,
  "preflight_runs_on_contains_blacksmith": true,
  "security_fast_needs_ci_scope": true,
  "security_fast_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate": true,
  "pnpm_warmup_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate": true,
  "check_docs_runs_on_contains_docs_only_gate": true
}
```

**Observed result:** All 11 assertions confirm the scope gate is properly wired: `ci_scope` runs on `ubuntu-24.04`, outputs `docs_only`, and `preflight`/`security-fast`/`pnpm-store-warmup`/`check-docs` all check the gate before choosing a runner.

**Not tested:** Actual CI run with a Markdown-only PR (requires pushing to GitHub). The workflow guard test validates the YAML structure, and the `node -e` verification confirms the parsed routing logic.

target.title: fix(google): use stable Gemini image model
target.body:
Google's preview Gemini image IDs and legacy Imagen 4 IDs are being retired, but OpenClaw still advertised preview/retired IDs across the Google/OpenRouter image-generation defaults and compatibility migration paths. This keeps stable Flash as the default while preserving explicit Pro selections by mapping Pro preview configs and provider catalogs to the stable Pro image IDs.

## Summary
- switch Google and OpenRouter image-generation provider defaults to `gemini-3.1-flash-image`
- preserve stable Pro image models in Google/OpenRouter provider catalogs and lightweight metadata
- migrate Flash preview/Imagen refs to stable Flash, while migrating Pro preview refs to stable Pro
- update image-generation docs, provider/tool tests, and doctor migration coverage

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: OpenClaw's real image provider/model selection surface should no longer advertise retired Google Gemini preview image IDs or legacy Imagen 4 IDs as defaults, and should not silently collapse explicit Pro image selections to Flash.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR commit `c4ba453f55`, using the real source runner and local OpenClaw setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=true node scripts/run-node.mjs infer image providers --json`
- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the real provider listing command showed Google and OpenRouter now expose stable Flash defaults plus stable Pro catalog entries:

```json
{
  "id": "google",
  "label": "Google",
  "defaultModel": "gemini-3.1-flash-image",
  "models": [
    "gemini-3.1-flash-image",
    "gemini-3-pro-image"
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "id": "openrouter",
  "label": "OpenRouter",
  "defaultModel": "google/gemini-3.1-flash-image",
  "models": [
    "google/gemini-3.1-flash-image",
    "google/gemini-3-pro-image",
    "openai/gpt-5.4-image-2"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The runtime provider list reports stable Flash defaults while preserving distinct stable Pro image model entries for Google and OpenRouter. The retired `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, and Imagen 4 IDs are not advertised in the runtime provider list.
- What was not tested: I did not submit a paid live image generation request to Google/OpenRouter from this PR branch. The provider request paths are covered by the targeted runtime/provider/tool tests listed below.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The first `node scripts/run-node.mjs infer image providers --json` attempt failed before execution because stale dist triggered a noninteractive pnpm install prompt. Rerunning with `CI=true` allowed the source runner to rebuild and complete the real provider-list command.
- Before evidence: Before this PR, Google/OpenRouter image defaults were preview IDs. In the first PR revision, Flash defaults were stable but explicit Pro preview refs were incorrectly migrated to Flash and Pro catalog entries were removed. This revision preserves Pro as stable Pro.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openrouter/image-generation-provider.test.ts src/image-generation/runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/media-generation/runtime-shared.test.ts src/config/plugin-auto-enable.core.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

- `CI=true node scripts/run-node.mjs infer image providers --json`

target.title: feat: add devcontainer for cross-platform development
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw cannot be fully developed on Windows — shell-based tests and setup scripts require Linux.
- Why it matters: Contributors on Windows need a portable, reproducible dev environment.

target.title: feat: fire session reset hooks for daily and idle resets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Internal hooks and `before_reset` plugin hook only fire for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands, not for daily (4AM) or idle-timeout session resets. This means `session-memory` never saves on daily/idle resets.
- Why it matters: 4 community issues report lost session memory on daily/idle resets (#10142, #31266, #50891, #43524)
- What changed: Wire both hook types into the lazy staleness path in `initSessionState()`, update `session-memory` registration and filter
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No new hook event names, no changes to plugin SDK API, no extraction/refactor — only `export` added to existing functions

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10142
- Closes #31266
- Closes #50891
- Closes #43524
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `initSessionState()` fires `session_end`/`session_start` plugin hooks for lazy resets but never fires `triggerInternalHook` or `hookRunner.runBeforeReset`. These only fire via `emitResetCommandHooks()` in `commands-core.ts`, which is only called for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted that internal hooks fire for lazy resets
- Contributing context (if known): The lazy evaluation design means sessions aren't proactively killed — staleness is checked on next message arrival, a code path separate from manual commands

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session.stale-hooks.test.ts` (new), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` (extended)
- Scenario the test should lock in: Internal hook fires with `action: "daily"` / `"idle"` when session is stale; `before_reset` plugin hook fires for lazy resets; no double-fire on manual reset
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Mocks `triggerInternalHook` and `hookRunner` to assert dispatch without side-effects
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `session-hooks-context.test.ts` covers `session_end`/`session_start` but not internal hooks or `before_reset`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session-memory` now saves session summaries on daily (4AM) and idle-timeout resets, not just manual `/new` and `/reset`. Plugin `before_reset` hook now fires for lazy resets, giving plugins pre-archive transcript access.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[lazy reset] -> session_end + session_start hooks only

After:
[lazy reset] -> internal hook (command:daily/idle) + before_reset + session_end + session_start
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Any
- Runtime/container: Node.js 24
- Integration/channel (if any): Any channel with daily or idle reset configured

### Steps

1. Configure a session with daily reset (default 4AM)
2. Have an active session with messages before 4AM
3. Send a message after 4AM (triggering lazy staleness detection)

### Expected

- `session-memory` saves a summary of the previous session
- `before_reset` plugin hook fires with `reason: "daily"` and transcript messages

### Actual (before this PR)

- `session-memory` does not fire — no summary saved
- `before_reset` plugin hook does not fire

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Daily reset with stale session triggers session-memory save; idle reset triggers save; manual /new does not double-fire
- Edge cases checked: First-ever session (no previous entry), system events (heartbeat), fresh session
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end with real LLM slug generation (mocked in tests)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `before_reset` receives the post-archive `sessionFile` path (after `renameSync`), not the original path. `loadBeforeResetTranscript` handles ENOENT by scanning for the latest `.reset.*` archived sibling.
  - Mitigation: This matches the manual reset path behavior. The stable post-archive path is intentional — it avoids racing transcript reads against the archive rename.

## AI Disclosure

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description
- [x] Note the degree of testing: fully tested — 10 unit tests (7 new + 3 extended) + manual daily/idle reset verification
- [x] Confirm: I understand what this code does and have verified the hook dispatch logic, guard conditions, and fire-and-forget patterns

🤖 AI-assisted with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(copilot): guard against undefined runtime.state during cli-metadata registration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94516: `@openclaw/copilot` crashes at startup with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'openSyncKeyedStore')`.

Two-layer fix:

**Layer B (api-builder.ts)** — Universal safety net. All plugins are protected from a bare `{}` runtime regardless of registration path.

**Layer C (copilot/index.ts)** — Mode-aware guard. Copilot is a full-runtime plugin (registers an agent harness), so metadata-only paths (`cli-metadata`, `discovery`, `setup-only`) skip registration. The full gateway activation path will pick it up later. Uses `api.registrationMode !== "full"` (the public SDK contract) instead of checking runtime internals.

**Layer — (loader.ts)** The existing `createMetadataRuntime()` stub provides a real in-memory store for metadata paths that do need `openSyncKeyedStore`.

## Why This Change Was Made

ClawSweeper review (#95229) requested:
- [P1] Gate Copilot registration by `api.registrationMode` instead of `runtime.state` shape
- [P1] "updating api" — ensure `runtime.state` is never undefined

This PR implements both, plus retains the loader-level stub.

## Evidence

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/copilot/index.test.ts`

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] No new dependencies
- [x] No config surface changes
- [x] No API contract changes
- [x] Copilot tests pass
- [x] Full-runtime failures stay loud (throws clear errors, not silent undefined)
- [x] Additive — existing runtime paths untouched

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review / ClawSweeper re-review

What is still waiting?
- CI (Crabbox) on maintainer approval

Which bot comments were addressed?
- [P2] Gate by `api.registrationMode !== "full"` → done
- [P1] Safe `runtime.state` in `buildPluginApi` → done

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Fix copilot registration crash when `runtime.state` is absent on metadata-only registration paths

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13.1, Linux x86_64

**Command run**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/copilot/index.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
```

**Observed result**: 5/5 tests pass. The `registrationMode` guard correctly skips non-full paths, and the `safeRuntime` proxy in `buildPluginApi` prevents any future plugin from hitting `undefined` state.

**What was not tested**: Full `openclaw plugins list` with npm-installed copilot (no registry access)
